execution_rules_5.rule: Every MCP server file under packages/jade-slack-channel/ MUST declare the `claude/channel` capability in its manifest and export a `channelCapability` const typed as `{ readonly name: "claude/channel"; readonly version: "2025-01-01" }`.

All Slack event payloads received by the channel plugin MUST be validated against a Zod schema before dispatch. Raw Slack JSON never reaches agent session code — only branded `SlackChannelEvent` discriminated unions cross the boundary.

Hook scripts (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop) are plain executables under `hooks/` returning JSON to stdout. They MUST exit 0 on success, exit 2 to block a tool call (PreToolUse only), and never throw — all errors are `Result<T, HookError>`.

The sender allowlist for each agent channel is stored in Neon (`channel_routing.allowed_senders jsonb`) and cached in-process with a 60-second TTL. Messages from senders not on the allowlist are silently dropped and audit-logged with reason `"sender_rejected"`.

Neon schema migrations for this addendum live in `packages/jade-knowledge-workers/src/migrations/` and follow the existing `NNNN_description.sql` naming. Each migration is idempotent (uses `IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE OR REPLACE`).

Audit log rows (`agent_audit_log`) are append-only. No UPDATE or DELETE statements may target this table. The table uses `timestamptz` for `created_at` and includes `session_id`, `agent_id`, `event_type`, `payload jsonb`, and `channel_id`.

Permission relay payloads (`notifications/claude/permissionRequest`) MUST include `meta.agent_id`, `meta.department`, and `meta.source_channel` so the Slack approver sees full context before granting tool use.

Headless mode (`--headless` flag or `JADECLI_HEADLESS=1` env) skips all interactive prompts, reads the sender allowlist from Neon, and auto-approves tools listed in the agent's `allowed_tools` column. Tools not on the list are denied with exit 2.

Every new exported function MUST have a corresponding vitest test. Channel plugin tests use `msw` (Mock Service Worker) to stub Slack API calls; hook tests use fixture JSON files under `__fixtures__/`.
Commit messages for this addendum use scope `channels` — e.g., `feat(channels): add Slack channel MCP server skeleton`. Linear references use `Closes JAD-NNN` in the commit footer.

The ship report (task 132) requires ALL prior tasks 113-131 to pass CI (typecheck + build + test + lint) on a single PR before the report is generated. The report is a Linear document, not a markdown file in the repo.

task.content:
Create jade-slack-channel package skeleton with MCP channel capability.
Initialize `packages/jade-slack-channel/` as a new workspace package:
1. `package.json` — name `@jadecli/jade-slack-channel`, peer deps on `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.12.0`, `@slack/web-api ^7.8.0`, `@slack/socket-mode ^2.0.0`, `zod ^3.24.0`. Add `"bin": { "jade-slack-channel": "./dist/cli.js" }`.
2. `tsconfig.json` — extends root, strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true.
3. `src/capability.ts`: ```typescript export const CHANNEL_CAPABILITY = { name: "claude/channel", version: "2025-01-01", } as const satisfies { readonly name: "claude/channel"; readonly version: string }; ```
4. `src/types.ts` — branded types: SlackChannelId, SlackUserId, SlackThreadTs, ChannelRouteId. Discriminated union `SlackChannelEvent = SlackMessageEvent | SlackReactionEvent | SlackThreadReplyEvent | SlackAppMentionEvent` with `readonly kind:` literal discriminant.
5. Add package to root tsconfig references and root package.json workspaces.
Commit: `feat(channels): scaffold jade-slack-channel package with MCP channel capability` Linear: Closes JAD-220
task.activeForm: Scaffolding jade-slack-channel package with channel capability declaration

task.content:
Implement Slack event ingestion and Zod validation layer.
In `packages/jade-slack-channel/src/`:
1. `schemas.ts` — Zod schemas for: slackMessageSchema, slackReactionSchema, slackAppMentionSchema, slackThreadReplySchema. All schemas produce branded types from task 113.
2. `event-parser.ts`: `export function parseSlackEvent(raw: unknown): Result<SlackChannelEvent, SlackParseError>` Dispatches on raw.type/raw.subtype, returns Result.ok() or Result.err() with structured SlackParseError.
3. `__tests__/event-parser.test.ts` — at least 12 tests: valid events, missing fields, malformed IDs, null/undefined/empty inputs.
Commit: `feat(channels): add Slack event Zod schemas and parser with Result monad` Linear: Closes JAD-221
task.activeForm: Implementing Slack event Zod validation and parser returning Result monad

task.content:
Build MCP server core with channel notification dispatch.
1. `server.ts` — `JadeSlackChannelServer` class: - Constructor takes ServerConfig { slackBotToken, slackAppToken, neonConnectionString, senderAllowlistTtlMs? } - Registers claude/channel capability - Implements notifications/claude/channel push with meta: { agent_id, department, source_channel } - Exposes MCP tools: slack_reply(channel_id, thread_ts, text), slack_react(channel_id, ts, emoji), slack_list_channels()
2. `router.ts` — ChannelRouter class: routeToAgent(channelId) returns AgentRouteInfo { agentId, department, sessionConfig } isAllowedSender(channelId, userId) checks Neon with 60s TTL cache
3. `__tests__/server.test.ts` — test capability registration, notification shape, tool schemas.
Commit: `feat(channels): implement MCP server with channel notifications and routing` Linear: Closes JAD-222
task.activeForm: Building MCP channel server with notification dispatch and agent routing

task.content:
Implement sender allowlist with Neon-backed cache.
1. `allowlist.ts` — SenderAllowlist class: check(channelId, userId) → Result<AllowlistDecision> AllowlistDecision = { allowed: true, reason: "on_list" } | { allowed: false, reason: "sender_rejected" | "channel_not_found" | "allowlist_empty" } In-memory Map cache keyed by SlackChannelId, entries expire after ttlMs. AllowlistCacheStats tracks hit/miss/eviction counts.
2. `__tests__/allowlist.test.ts` — 8+ tests: allowed/rejected/unknown channel, cache hit/miss/expiry, empty allowlist, concurrent request coalescing.
Commit: `feat(channels): add sender allowlist with TTL cache backed by Neon` Linear: Closes JAD-223
task.activeForm: Implementing sender allowlist with Neon-backed TTL cache

task.content:
Build permission relay bridge for remote tool approval via Slack.
1. `permission-relay.ts` — PermissionRelay class: requestApproval(request) → Result<PermissionRelayResponse> handleSlackAction(action) handles Block Kit button clicks Posts Slack Block Kit message with agent name, department, tool name, sanitized input preview, Approve/Deny buttons. Waits up to 120s for response, returns timeout if none.
2. Wire into MCP server: intercept notifications/claude/permissionRequest, delegate to PermissionRelay.
3. `__tests__/permission-relay.test.ts` — approve flow, deny flow, timeout, malformed action.
Commit: `feat(channels): add permission relay bridge for Slack-based tool approval` Linear: Closes JAD-224
task.activeForm: Building permission relay bridge for remote tool approval through Slack

task.content:
Add CLI entry point and plugin manifest for jade-slack-channel.
1. `src/cli.ts` — parses env vars (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN, NEON_CONNECTION_STRING, JADECLI_HEADLESS), validates, instantiates server, connects Socket Mode, starts MCP stdio transport. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
2. `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` — name, version, description, capabilities: ["claude/channel"], entrypoint.
3. `__tests__/cli.test.ts` — missing env var exits 1, valid env vars create server.
Commit: `feat(channels): add CLI entry point and plugin manifest for Slack channel server` Linear: Closes JAD-225
task.activeForm: Adding CLI entry point and Claude Code plugin manifest for Slack channel server

task.content:
Create SessionStart hook for agent persona injection.
1. `hooks/session-start.ts` — reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and JADE_AGENT_ID from env, queries Neon for agent record (name, department, system_prompt, persona_traits, allowed_tools), assembles systemPromptPrefix from persona traits and department instructions. Outputs JSON with systemPromptPrefix, agentId, department, persona, allowedTools. Exit 0 on success, exit 1 on error.
2. `hooks/types.ts` — shared hook types: HookExitCode, HookError, PreToolUseOutput, PostToolUseOutput, StopOutput.
3. `.claude/settings.json` hook registration for SessionStart.
4. `hooks/__tests__/session-start.test.ts` — persona assembly, missing agent ID, DB failure.
Commit: `feat(channels): add SessionStart hook for agent persona injection from Neon` Linear: Closes JAD-226
task.activeForm: Creating SessionStart hook that injects agent persona from Neon into Claude session

task.content:
Implement PreToolUse hook for agent-scoped tool gating.
1. `hooks/pre-tool-use.ts` — reads stdin { tool_name, tool_input, session_id }, reads JADE_AGENT_ID from env, loads allowed_tools from Neon (60s TTL cache). Decision: allowed tool → exit 0; disallowed + headless → exit 2 (block); disallowed + interactive → exit 0 (defer to Claude prompt). Own channel tools (mcp__jade-slack-channel__*) always allowed.
2. `__tests__/pre-tool-use.test.ts` — 10+ tests covering all decision paths, cache, error cases.
Commit: `feat(channels): add PreToolUse hook for agent-scoped tool gating` Linear: Closes JAD-227
task.activeForm: Implementing PreToolUse hook for tool gating based on agent allowed-tools list

task.content:
Implement PostToolUse hook for audit logging.
1. `hooks/post-tool-use.ts` — reads stdin { tool_name, tool_input, tool_output, duration_ms, session_id }, writes to agent_audit_log table. tool_input_hash is SHA-256. payload contains tool_name, duration_ms, output_size_bytes, success — never raw output. Fire-and-forget: DB failure logs to stderr but still exits 0.
2. `__tests__/post-tool-use.test.ts` — successful write, DB failure resilience, hash correctness, payload sanitization.
Commit: `feat(channels): add PostToolUse hook for audit logging to Neon` Linear: Closes JAD-228
task.activeForm: Implementing PostToolUse hook for append-only audit logging to Neon

task.content:
Implement Stop hook for session summary and cost tracking.
1. `hooks/stop.ts` — reads stdin { session_id, stop_reason, usage }, calculates cost via existing calculateCost(), writes to agent_session_log, posts summary to Slack channel if one exists. Exit 0 always.
2. `__tests__/stop.test.ts` — cost calculation, summary formatting, DB write, Slack post (mocked), error resilience.
Commit: `feat(channels): add Stop hook for session summary and cost tracking` Linear: Closes JAD-229
task.activeForm: Implementing Stop hook for session summary generation and cost tracking

task.content:
Create hooks settings generator and integration test suite.
1. `src/hooks-config.ts` — generateHooksSettings(options) returns Claude Code settings.json hooks schema with all 4 hooks registered with configurable timeouts.
2. `__tests__/hooks-integration.test.ts` — end-to-end: spawn each hook as child process, pipe test JSON to stdin, assert stdout/exit codes. Full lifecycle: SessionStart → PreToolUse (allow) → PostToolUse → Stop.
3. Add "hooks:build" script to package.json.
Commit: `feat(channels): add hooks settings generator and integration test suite` Linear: Closes JAD-230
task.activeForm: Creating hooks settings generator and running full lifecycle integration tests

task.content:
Add Neon migration for channel_routing table.
1. `migrations/0005_channel_routing.sql` — channel_routing table with: id, agent_id (FK), slack_channel_id (unique), slack_channel_name, allowed_senders (jsonb), session_config (jsonb), is_active, timestamps. Indexes on slack_channel_id and agent_id where active. Auto-update trigger for updated_at.
2. CRUD functions in `src/channel-routing.ts`: createChannelRoute, getChannelRouteBySlackChannel, getChannelRouteByAgent, updateChannelRoute, deactivateChannelRoute.
3. `__tests__/channel-routing.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add Neon migration and CRUD for channel_routing table` Linear: Closes JAD-231
task.activeForm: Adding Neon migration for channel_routing table with CRUD functions

task.content:
Add Neon migration for agent_audit_log table.
1. `migrations/0006_agent_audit_log.sql` — append-only table: id (bigserial), agent_id, session_id, channel_id, event_type (constrained enum), tool_name, tool_input_hash, duration_ms, payload (jsonb), created_at. Indexes on agent+created, session, event_type. REVOKE UPDATE,DELETE FROM PUBLIC.
2. Query functions in `src/audit-log.ts`: appendAuditEntry, queryAuditLog(filter), getAgentAuditSummary(agentId, since).
3. `__tests__/audit-log.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add Neon migration and query functions for agent_audit_log` Linear: Closes JAD-232
task.activeForm: Adding Neon migration for append-only agent_audit_log table

task.content:
Add Neon migration for agent_session_log and metrics views.
1. `migrations/0007_agent_session_log.sql` — session log table + materialized view agent_daily_metrics (session_count, total_tokens, total_cost_usd, avg_tokens_per_session per agent per day).
2. Functions in `src/session-log.ts`: insertSessionLog, getAgentDailyMetrics(agentId, from, to), refreshDailyMetrics.
3. `__tests__/session-log.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add session log table and daily metrics materialized view` Linear: Closes JAD-233
task.activeForm: Adding Neon migration for agent_session_log and daily metrics materialized view

task.content:
Extend agents table with channel and hook configuration columns.
1. `migrations/0008_agents_channel_columns.sql` — ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN: persona_traits (jsonb), allowed_tools (jsonb), hook_overrides (jsonb), slack_channel_name (text), is_channel_enabled (boolean). Backfill allowed_tools by department.
2. Update Agent type in types.ts with new fields.
3. Update CRUD functions in agents.ts to include new columns.
4. `__tests__/agents-channel.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): extend agents table with channel config and allowed_tools columns` Linear: Closes JAD-234
task.activeForm: Extending agents table with persona traits, allowed tools, and channel configuration

task.content:
Implement headless mode for CI and scheduled automation.
1. `src/headless.ts` — HeadlessController class: isHeadless() checks JADECLI_HEADLESS=1, autoApprove(toolName, agentId) checks agent's allowed_tools from Neon, getSessionFlags() returns CLI flags for spawning headless sessions.
2. Update cli.ts to read JADECLI_HEADLESS and pass HeadlessConfig.
3. `__tests__/headless.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add headless mode controller for CI and scheduled automation` Linear: Closes JAD-235
task.activeForm: Implementing headless mode controller for CI and scheduled agent automation

task.content:
Create scheduled task definitions for agent health checks and metrics refresh.
1. `src/scheduled-tasks.ts` — generateScheduledTaskDefinitions(agents) returns: - agent-health-check (*/15 * * * *): ping channels, verify Neon, report unhealthy - metrics-refresh (0 * * * *): refresh materialized view, post daily summary at 9am - audit-log-digest (0 8 * * *): 24h audit summary per department - stale-session-cleanup (0 2 * * *): mark orphaned sessions as error
2. `src/provision-tasks.ts` — writes SKILL.md files to ~/.claude/scheduled-tasks/.
3. `__tests__/scheduled-tasks.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add scheduled task definitions for health checks and metrics` Linear: Closes JAD-236
task.activeForm: Creating scheduled task definitions for agent health checks and metrics refresh

task.content:
Build channel provisioning script for bulk agent-to-Slack-channel setup.
1. `src/provision-channels.ts` — provisionAgentChannels(db, slackClient, options) creates Slack channels for each enabled agent, sets topics, inserts channel_routing rows, updates agent slack_channel_name. Dry-run mode available.
2. `scripts/provision-channels.ts` — CLI wrapper.
3. `__tests__/provision-channels.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add bulk channel provisioning script for agent-to-Slack setup` Linear: Closes JAD-237
task.activeForm: Building bulk channel provisioning script for agent-to-Slack-channel mapping

task.content:
Add remote control integration for interactive debugging of agent sessions.
1. `src/remote-control.ts` — RemoteControl class with commands: inspect, override_tool, inject_context, terminate. SessionStatus discriminated union: running | paused | stopped. Commands delivered via MCP channel notification. Slack slash command /jade-debug integration.
2. All remote control actions audit-logged with event_type: "remote_command".
3. `__tests__/remote-control.test.ts`.
Commit: `feat(channels): add remote control for interactive agent session debugging` Linear: Closes JAD-238
task.activeForm: Adding remote control integration for interactive debugging of running agent sessions

task.content:
Generate ship report and create release PR for Channels integration.
Prerequisites: ALL tasks 113-131 pass CI.
1. Run: npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test && npm run lint — all must pass. 2. Verify 120+ tests passing. 3. Create Linear document "Channels Integration Ship Report": - Packages: @jadecli/jade-slack-channel (new), @jadecli/jade-knowledge-workers (extended) - Tables: channel_routing, agent_audit_log, agent_session_log, agent_daily_metrics - Hooks: SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop - Capabilities: sender allowlist, permission relay, headless mode, scheduled tasks, remote control 4. Create PR to main with all JAD-220 through JAD-238 references.
Commit: `docs(channels): add ship report for Channels x Knowledge Workers integration` Linear: Closes JAD-239
task.activeForm: Generating ship report and creating release PR for Channels integration
